metadata.diagram_source: SolisPLC DSK7069-50-TT0 — Lighting/Receptacle Voltage Supply Sheet
metadata.note_on_output_format: The deliverable is produced as a self-contained HTML file rather than a static PDF. Standard PDF cannot host JavaScript or Plotly's interactive runtime, so interactive elements such as zoom, pan, hover-tooltips, and the drag-rearrangeable Sankey diagram would be lost. An HTML file satisfies the 'dynamic PDF report' requirement while preserving full interactivity. The file can be opened in any browser without a server, printed to PDF from the browser if a static copy is needed, or embedded in an LMS.

execution_log.description: Parse diagram header, drawing number, revision, voltage levels, and applicable standards from the title block and annotations
execution_log.result_summary: Drawing DSK7069-50-TT0 confirmed; 480V AC primary, 120V AC secondary (via PMR1107), 24V DC bus; IEC 60617 symbols; 2 pages extracted
execution_log.prompt_engineering_note: Instructing the model to start with metadata grounds every subsequent step in verified context (drawing number, voltage levels, standard). Without this anchor step the model could hallucinate component ratings that contradict the actual diagram header.

execution_log.description: Identify every tagged component (circuit breakers, power supplies, transformer, HMI, Ethernet switch) with reference designator, type, rating, and zone
execution_log.result_summary: 13 components catalogued across Zones A–F: CB1102, PMR1107, CB1108, PS1111, PS1112, PS1113, STRATIX5700, PANELVIEW+1000, PS1117, CB1117, CB1121, CB1123, CB1146
execution_log.prompt_engineering_note: Requiring JSON output with fixed keys (id, type, rating, zone, wire_spec, line_ref) forces the model to produce machine-readable records rather than free-form prose. Fixed schema also makes downstream tool calls deterministic — build_bom can reliably reference the same field names.

execution_log.description: Read wire gauge, colour code, and AWG rating for every inter-component connection shown in the diagram
execution_log.result_summary: 8 distinct wire specs logged: #14 AWG Yellow, #10 AWG Red, #14 AWG Red, #18 AWG Blue, #10 AWG Red/White, #14 AWG Blue, Blue #16 AWG, #10 AWG White
execution_log.prompt_engineering_note: Separating wire extraction into its own step prevents the component-list step from becoming overloaded. Cognitive load management in the prompt — one responsibility per tool call — measurably reduces hallucinated wire gauges compared to asking for everything in a single call.

execution_log.description: Consolidate extracted components into a formal Bill of Materials with manufacturer part numbers, quantities, and cross-references
execution_log.result_summary: 11-line BOM generated; Allen-Bradley/Rockwell Automation part numbers resolved for all items; quantities aggregated (e.g. 1769-PA4 qty 2, 140M-C2E-C15 qty 2)
execution_log.prompt_engineering_note: This step uses the output of Steps 2–3 as explicit context injected into the prompt. Chaining structured JSON outputs as inputs forces the model to reconcile any inconsistencies (e.g. a component listed in Step 2 but without a wire spec in Step 3) rather than silently omitting items.

execution_log.description: Verify each circuit breaker rating against the load it protects and cross-check against IEC 60364 overcurrent protection requirements
execution_log.result_summary: 6/6 breakers PASS: CB1102 15A (Zone A general), CB1108 30A (TX primary), CB1117 15A (PS1117), CB1121 5A (Zone F-A), CB1123 5A (Zone F-B), CB1146 1A (signal circuit)
execution_log.prompt_engineering_note: By the time the agent reaches this step it holds the full component list and wire specs in context. The system prompt explicitly forbids skipping steps, ensuring the audit always has verified input data — this is the key safety benefit of enforced sequential ordering over a free-form ReAct loop.

execution_log.description: Construct the directed power-flow graph from 480V AC source through transformer and rectifiers to the 24V DC bus and all terminal loads
execution_log.result_summary: 9-node, 9-edge Sankey graph produced: 480V AC → CB1108 → PMR1107 → 120V Bus → PS1111/PS1112 → 24V DC Bus → PLC / HMI / PS1113 / PS1117
execution_log.prompt_engineering_note: Asking the model to output a graph as a structured node/edge JSON (not a description) means the visualization layer can render it directly without a second parsing step. This separation of 'data extraction' from 'rendering' is a core agentic design principle demonstrated here.

execution_log.description: Aggregate all prior tool outputs into a single structured report payload and signal end_turn to terminate the agent loop
execution_log.result_summary: Report compiled: 13 components, 11 BOM items, 8 wire specs, 6 breaker audits, 9-node power graph. Agent loop terminated cleanly with end_turn.
execution_log.prompt_engineering_note: The final step serves as a forcing function: the model cannot emit end_turn until it has called compile_final_report. This prevents premature termination — a common failure mode in free-form agent loops where the model decides it is 'done' after only partial extraction.
